content:
I want to be able to switch a large chunk of my email archive "off" so it doesn't automatically load when Thunderbird launches

I don't think that's possible, and it wouldn't make any sense to me. Thunderbird doesn't have a problem with large profiles, and it doesn't load all messages in all folders at startup. However, the no. of messages in a single folder can be more problematic. So you may have a problem with very large folders, and with a large no. of messages in the Local Folders account. It would be better to split an archive in multiple smaller folders with less messages in them, e.g. on a yearly basis.

Also, you may want to create an exception in your anti-virus software for the Thunderbird profile folder, so that the anti-virus real-time scanner will not scan it. At best, anti-virus software can slow down Thunderbird. At worst, it can corrupt mail files and cause data loss.

Wrt the high memory usage, some quick items:

  • Try Help > Troubleshoot Mode.
  • Do you have any folders where you've seen problems?
  • Do you have many newsgroup or rss accounts, or virtual folders?

For a longer list, see https://wiki.mozilla.org/Thunderbird:Testing:Memory_Usage_Problems
creator: christ1

content: Just a comment here: In gmail, all mail is in Allmail, and that can be confusing. If you find the mail online and the headers show the recipient, then the message was sent. Also, with Gmail, a particular message may be cataloged in multiple labels (folders). The general recommendation in Thunderbird is to NOT use the Allmail folder: it causes confusion, doubles disk space, doubles transfer resource usage. On your specific issue, I do not know why the messages do not appear where you expected them.

content:
The -- has been around for decades as a separator between text and signature. The intent was a pseudo-standard that allowed email clients to automatically remove sigs on replies to avoid clutter of seeing a sig multiple times on a long thread. More info on that is here: https://mailformat.dan.info/trailers/sigblocks.html

You can permanently remove it from instructions on this site below:

https://kb.mozillazine.org/Signatures_-_Thunderbird#Removing_the_signature_separator
creator: davidsk

content:
Oh, okay. Been using Outlook and hardly used it (no heavy office use). Thought it was a weird Thunderbird feature.

I did find an article that said something about "mail.strip_sig_on_reply" but you know the actual proper setting: mail.identity.default.suppress_signature_separator

Thanks for the assistance David and the back info. If I ever come across "--" again, I'll know what it's for.

david said

content:
Thunderbird failed to find the settings for your email account.

When Thunderbird cannot find the server settings for your account use manual account setup. For TWC/Spectrum there seem to be a lot of different server settings, so this is a little bit trial and error.

Try these for manual setup. Also turn of the experimental Account Hub in case it is enabled in your TB Settings: At the top right of the Thunderbird window, click the menu button ≡ > Account Settings > Settings > General > Account Hub Un-check 'Create accounts in the new Account Hub'

For the incoming server: Server type: IMAP Server name: mail.twc.com Connection security: SSL/TLS Port: 993 Authentication method: Normal password

For the outgoing server: Server type: SMTP Server name: mail.twc.com Connection security: SSL/TLS Port: 465 Authentication method: Normal password

If these won't work also see https://www.roadrunneremail-rr.com/time-warner-cable-twc-imap-pop3-smtp-email-settings https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1161629 https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1297771

Another potential problem:

Antivirus: McAfee

McAfee has been problematic in connection with Thunderbird in the past. If you're still running into problems try to start Windows 11 in safe mode with networking enabled. Does the problem go away?

Windows safe mode disables anti-virus software. This is a troubleshooting step, not a solution.

content:
quanton said
It is surprisingly dysfunctional when trying to find messages from a specific entity/person...

"Renewing" the global search index from time to time can improve it's efficacy. Go to Help > Troubleshooting information > Profile folder > Open folder, then quit Thunderbird . In the profile folder search for the global-messages-db.sqlite file and delete it. Close the profile folder and restart TB which will rebuild the GLODA; be patient because this can take some time....

Especially things that are not really database dependant, eg: Providing an option for a Saved Search to automatically include subfolders without the user having to select and check every single one manually. A tick next to a folder, AND a tick in an option entitled "Include all subfolders of selected folders" would be massively helpful!

content:
I suspect a corrupt message, in particular, it might be missing the Date: and or other required header lines. It could have been sent to you in this condition.

While viewing the message, press Ctrl+U to view the raw message data, or go to menu item (on Windows) <View | Headers | All > to see the raw headers. Your may not know exactly what to look for, but does anything seem to be missing or garbled, especially the Date: line?

content:
Many thanks Lin. Unfortunately this is complicated by time zones. Msg received in New Zealand from England (13 hours behind NZ at this time of year). The local displayed time for the msg in TB currently sits at 20.57, yet I had replied to that msg 4 hours earlier!

Raw data (Ctrl-U) shows From as Feb 21 13.54. Would that be my local time of receipt? If so then that is the correct time for the msg. I replied to it at 16.14, yet as already mentioned TB displays the time as 20.57.

In the raw data the Received and X-Received time/date are PST. Not sure why that should be and not either GMT or NZDST. Possibly the arrival time on the Gmail server?

Any further ideas to correct this annoyance would be most welcome. Thanks again for your help.
creator: newamalfi

content:
I know only a little about how to interpret email headers, but I do know that the From line does not normally contain a date, so if yours does, that's a red flag in of itself. The important question is, does it contain a Date line?

I believe that when you create a message, the current date and time are stamped in the Date header. Each server it passes through on its way to the destination stamps that current date/time in a Received line, oldest toward the bottom of the header block, newest toward the top.

A quick experiment with Thunderbird seems to indicate that if the originating/creation Date line is missing or malformed, Tbird considers the time received to be the sending time, which, of course, can be wildly wrong. The date shown is not actually found in the header, so I think it must be stored in the index. Furthermore, if I move one of those messages to another folder, it will now show that date it was moved as the sending time!

I'm not sure exactly what this all means, but I have a feeling that it's related to your issue of dates that seem to change unpredictably.

I might try creating a new folder, copying all messages in the errant folder into it, and examine the new folder to see it it appears more stable. If so, delete the old folder. You may need to adjust any filters that reference the old folder.

content:
I agree, the source of the problem is probably that particular sender. Their email agent is neglecting the Date entirely or is creating malformed header information. Normally, the "From:" line contains only the email address and a short description, usually the name of the sender, in the form:

From: Mozilla Support Forum <no-reply@support.mozilla.org>

The separate "Date:" line should look similar to this:

Date: Mon, 23 Feb 2026 17:32:35 +0000

Without seeing your example, I get the impression that those two lines may be concatenated. The result is an invalid header field.

If it's important enough to you, you could export the message in .EML format, edit it with a text editor, correcting the headers, and import the fixed version. It might not be worth the bother.
